channel.title: Awards on Advanced Integration Laboratory
channel.description: Recent content in Awards on Advanced Integration Laboratory

item.title: Lab Teams Receive Honours at Engineering Research Project Competition
item.description: Two student teams from the lab received Honourable Mentions (佳作) at the College of Engineering Undergraduate Research Project Competition (專題研究成果競賽), with projects on an underwater bio-inspired stingray vehicle and a maple-seed-inspired unmanned aerial vehicle.

item.title: Lab Teams Excel at NUST Scientific Paper Competition
item.description: Two undergraduate teams from the laboratory shared First Place in the Engineering Technology category at the National University System of Taiwan (NUST, 臺灣國立大學系統) Scientific Paper Competition (科技論文競賽), with projects inspired by stingrays and maple seeds. A postgraduate team also received an Honorable Mention Award (佳作) for its dragonfly-inspired drone research.

item.title: Infinite Potential Award Secured at the 2025 Innovation Arena
item.description: Under the guidance of Dr Lan, a team of three undergraduate students received the Infinite Potential Award (潛力無限獎) at the 2025 Innovation Arena (興創競技場), organised by the Startup @NCHU Innovation Center. The team developed a biomimetic rotary-wing unmanned aerial vehicle inspired by natural flight mechanisms, highlighting the practical potential of university-driven innovation and applied research.

item.title: Dr Lan Receives Meritorious Research Award at TCUS Innovation Competition
item.description: Dr Lan received a Meritorious Research Award in the Young Scholars Innovation Competition organised by the Taiwan Comprehensive University System (臺灣綜合大學系統). His research combined biomimetic propulsion concepts with AI techniques to improve energy efficiency across aerial and aquatic environments, demonstrating the potential of nature-inspired multifunctional propulsion systems.

item.title: Qi-Sheng Huang Earns Honourable Mention at CSME Conference
item.description: At this year’s CSME Conference, Qi-Sheng Huang received an Honourable Mention (佳作) in the student paper competition for research supervised by Dr Lan. The study applied the silkmoth’s tracking behaviour to robotic guidance and control, achieving improved efficiency and accuracy through a nature-inspired approach.

item.title: Graduate Team Wins Merit Award in IMBD
item.description: A graduate student team supervised by Dr Lan, led by Yen-Cheng Chang alongside two fellow members, received a Merit Award (優等獎) in the National Intelligent Manufacturing Big Data Analytics Competition (IMBD, 全國智慧製造大數據分析競賽). Their project applied artificial intelligence and big data techniques to deliver innovative, data-driven solutions with strong analytical performance.

item.title: Undergraduate Team Receives Honourable Mention for Bio-Inspired Ray Project
item.description: Three undergraduate students supervised by Dr Lan received an Honourable Mention（佳作）in the Capstone Project (機械設計與製作專題) competition. Their project explored a bio-inspired ray system, applying ray-like propulsion principles to demonstrate the potential of biomimetic design in underwater robotics.

item.title: Ming-Han Wu Takes Third Place in AASRC Best Paper Award
item.description: Ming-Han Wu, with co-author Yi-Hsi Yeh, supervised by Dr Lan, has secured third place in the Best Paper Award competition at the AASRC Conference. Their study examined how variations in wing phase shift affect thrust generation in a robotic dragonfly, offering insights that contribute to ongoing research in bio-inspired flight.

item.title: Two Teams Win Maker Potential Award at the 2025 Makerthon
item.description: Two student teams supervised by Dr Lan received the Maker Potential Award (創客潜力獎) at the 2025 Makerthon – Technology Empowerment × Future-Making Competition (創客松-科技賦能x自造未來競賽). One team developed an aerial drone and the other a marine-inspired submersible vehicle, showcasing strong creativity and engineering capability.

item.title: 1st Place at the 2025 Ocean Challenge
item.description: Three undergraduates, supervised by Dr Lan, won 1st place at the 2025 Ocean Challenge (臺灣海洋國際青年論壇) with a manta ray-inspired underwater vehicle.

item.title: Nature-Inspired Designs Win Awards in Bionic Design Competition
item.description: Under the guidance of Dr Lan, two student teams received awards at the 2025 Bionic Design Competition (仿生設計競賽) for their nature-inspired engineering projects. One team won a Merit Award (優選) for a bio-inspired stingray underwater vehicle, while the other received an Honourable Mention (佳作) for a butterfly-inspired micro air vehicle, highlighting the potential of biomimetic design for sustainable innovation.

item.title: Dr Lan Awarded Outstanding Reviewer Award
item.description: Dr Lan has been honoured with the Outstanding Reviewer Award 2024, a prestigious accolade recognising his significant contributions to academic research. Congratulations!

item.title: Students Shine at the 2024 Innovation Arena
item.description: Under the guidance of Dr Lan, a team of four students received the Infinite Potential Award (潛力無限獎) in the Creative Concept category at the 2024 Innovation Arena (興創競技場). Organised by the University’s Centre for Industry-Academia Collaboration, the competition promotes the translation of academic research and innovative ideas into practical industry applications.

item.title: Hsing-Yun Huang and Thanh Kim Hua Earn Honours at CSME
item.description: Dr Lan’s students, Hsing-Yun Huang and Thanh Kim Hua, received Honourable Mentions (佳作) at the CSME Student Paper Competition for their research achievements. Their awards recognise innovative research contributions and reflect the strong academic mentorship within Dr Lan’s team.

item.title: Dr Lan Awarded IOP Trusted Reviewer Status
item.description: Dr Lan has been honoured with the IOP Trusted Reviewer Status, a prestigious accolade recognising his significant contributions to peer review. Congratulations!
